theme: move the toll booths, then shrink the engines
title: Move the Toll Booths, Shrink the Engines
seed_problem: sequential circuit sizing with a fixed register placement and a per-gate implementation menu
mutation: composite: couple discrete gate/engine sizing to a booth (register) relocation freedom along each pipeline under a total-conserved-per-pipeline budget, where relocation cost varies per belt -- so the cheap-vs-fast sizing choice and the checkpoint layout must be co-optimized, not solved as fixed-registers-then-upsize.
strategies: trivial: keep the original booth placement, run every station at its priciest/fastest variant (reproduces the checker baseline)

greedy: keep the original booth placement (no retiming), start every station at its cheapest variant, then repeatedly upsize the largest-delay station in any run that exceeds the clock -- the obvious 'fixed registers, size up the critical path' recipe

insight (strong): compute how many booths a cheapest-everywhere chunking of each pipeline would actually need against the clock; when the pipeline's conserved booth total covers that (the planted trap cases), relocate booths to the computed chunk boundaries and dump any spare booths on the cheapest-relocation belt, letting nearly the whole pipeline run on the cheap variant
invalid: emits an out-of-range engine-variant index (deliberately infeasible)
